input.content: I heard that the technology sector is booming these days. Get me a list of potential tech stocks I could invest in.

tools.name: authenticate_twitter
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Authenticate a user with username and password. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tools.name: comment
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Comment on a tweet for the authenticated user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tweet_id.description: ID of the tweet to comment on.

tools.name: follow_user
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Follow a user for the authenticated user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

username_to_follow.description: Username of the user to follow.

follow_status.description: True if followed, False if already following.

tools.name: get_tweet
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Retrieve a specific tweet. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tweet_id.description: ID of the tweet to retrieve.

tags.description: List of tags associated with the tweet.

mentions.description: List of users mentioned in the tweet.

tools.name: get_tweet_comments
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Retrieve all comments for a specific tweet. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tweet_id.description: ID of the tweet to retrieve comments for.

comments.description: List of dictionaries, each containing comment information.

tools.name: get_user_stats
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Get statistics for a specific user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

username.description: Username of the user to get statistics for.

tweet_count.description: Number of tweets posted by the user.

following_count.description: Number of users the specified user is following.

retweet_count.description: Number of retweets made by the user.

tools.name: get_user_tweets
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Retrieve all tweets from a specific user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

username.description: Username of the user whose tweets to retrieve.

user_tweets.description: List of dictionaries, each containing tweet information.

tags.description: List of tags associated with the tweet.

mentions.description: List of users mentioned in the tweet.

tools.name: list_all_following
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: List all users that the authenticated user is following. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

following_list.description: List of all users that the authenticated user is following.

tools.name: mention
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Mention specified users in a tweet. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tweet_id.description: ID of the tweet where users are mentioned.

mentioned_usernames.description: List of usernames to be mentioned.

tools.name: post_tweet
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Post a tweet for the authenticated user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tags.description: List of tags for the tweet. Tag name should start with #. This is only relevant if the user wants to add tags to the tweet.

mentions.description: List of users mentioned in the tweet. Mention name should start with @. This is only relevant if the user wants to add mentions to the tweet.

tags.description: List of tags associated with the tweet.

mentions.description: List of users mentioned in the tweet.

tools.name: posting_get_login_status
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Get the login status of the current user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

login_status.description: True if the current user is logged in, False otherwise.

tools.name: retweet
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Retweet a tweet for the authenticated user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tweet_id.description: ID of the tweet to retweet.

tools.name: search_tweets
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Search for tweets containing a specific keyword. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

keyword.description: Keyword to search for in the content of the tweets.

matching_tweets.description: List of dictionaries, each containing tweet information.

tags.description: List of tags associated with the tweet.

mentions.description: List of users mentioned in the tweet.

tools.name: unfollow_user
tools.description: This tool belongs to the TwitterAPI, which provides core functionality for posting tweets, retweeting, commenting, and following users on Twitter. Tool description: Unfollow a user for the authenticated user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

username_to_unfollow.description: Username of the user to unfollow.

unfollow_status.description: True if unfollowed, False if not following.

tools.name: add_to_watchlist
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Add a stock to the watchlist. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

stock.description: the stock symbol to add to the watchlist.

tools.name: cancel_order
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Cancel an order. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

order_id.description: ID of the order to cancel.

status.description: New status of the order after cancellation attempt.

tools.name: filter_stocks_by_price
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Filter stocks based on a price range. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

stocks.description: List of stock symbols to filter.

min_price.description: Minimum stock price. This is a float type value.

max_price.description: Maximum stock price.  This is a float type value.

filtered_stocks.description: Filtered list of stock symbols within the price range.

tools.name: fund_account
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Fund the account with the specified amount. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

amount.description: Amount to fund the account with.  This is a float type value.

tools.name: get_account_info
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get account information. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

binding_card.description: Card number associated with the account.

tools.name: get_available_stocks
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get a list of stock symbols in the given sector. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

sector.description: The sector to retrieve stocks from (e.g., 'Technology').

stock_list.description: List of stock symbols in the specified sector.

tools.name: get_current_time
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the current time. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

current_time.description: Current time in HH:MM AM/PM format.

tools.name: get_order_details
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the details of an order. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

symbol.description: Symbol of the stock in the order.

price.description: Price at which the order was placed.

amount.description: Number of shares in the order.

tools.name: get_order_history
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the stock order ID history. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

order_history.description: List of orders ID in the order history.

tools.name: get_stock_info
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the details of a stock. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

symbol.description: Symbol that uniquely identifies the stock.

tools.name: get_symbol_by_name
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the symbol of a stock by company name. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

symbol.description: Symbol of the stock or "Stock not found" if not available.

tools.name: get_transaction_history
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the transaction history within a specified date range. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

transaction_history.description: List of transactions within the specified date range.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the transaction, formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'.

tools.name: get_watchlist
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the watchlist. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

watchlist.description: List of stock symbols in the watchlist.

tools.name: notify_price_change
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Notify if there is a significant price change in the stocks. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

stocks.description: List of stock symbols to check.

threshold.description: Percentage change threshold to trigger a notification.  This is a float type value.

notification.description: Notification message about the price changes.

tools.name: place_order
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Place an order. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

symbol.description: Symbol of the stock to trade.

price.description: Price at which to place the order. This is a float type value.

order_id.description: ID of the newly placed order.

price.description: Price at which the order was placed.

amount.description: Number of shares in the order.

tools.name: remove_stock_from_watchlist
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Remove a stock from the watchlist. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

symbol.description: Symbol of the stock to remove.

tools.name: trading_get_login_status
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Get the login status. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tools.name: trading_login
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Handle user login. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tools.name: trading_logout
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Handle user logout for trading system. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tools.name: withdraw_funds
tools.description: This tool belongs to the trading system, which allows users to trade stocks, manage their account, and view stock information. Tool description: Withdraw funds from the account balance. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

amount.description: Amount to withdraw from the account.  This is a float type value.

new_balance.description: Updated account balance after the transaction.

messages.content:
[Golden Rule Compiler]
- For tool-enabled turns, emit the next tool call instead of explanatory prose whenever the next action is already supported by the available tools.
- Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
- Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
- When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
- Known explicit context values you can reuse exactly if relevant: stock_list, AAPL, GOOG, MSFT, NVDA
- When tools are available and the current request already contains enough local evidence to continue, emit the next tool call instead of ending the turn with explanatory prose.
- Do not end a tool-enabled turn with prose-only status updates when the next action can be grounded from the current request, prior explicit literals, or prior tool outputs.
- Reuse explicit file names, paths, identifiers, or quoted literals already present in the request context before asking for clarification or stopping.
- Use prior tool outputs as grounding for the next tool call instead of switching into natural-language explanation.
I heard that the technology sector is booming these days. Get me a list of potential tech stocks I could invest in.

message.content:
I've retrieved a list of technology sector stocks for you to consider investing in. The stocks are:

- AAPL (Apple Inc.)
- GOOG (Alphabet Inc.)
- MSFT (Microsoft Corporation)
- NVDA (NVIDIA Corporation)

If you'd like more detailed information on any of these stocks or if you want to add them to your watchlist, please let me know!

- NVDA (NVIDIA Corporation)

If you'd like more detailed information on any of these stocks or if you want to add them to your watchlist, please let me know!
